_doc: Auth-provider definitions for zelosMCP. Decoupled from the MCP
server catalog (configs/default-zelosmcp.json) so the two have
different sensitivity classes in remote deploys: this file is the
ConfigMap-vs-Secret 'Secret' half (provider IDs + scopes), the
server catalog is the ConfigMap half (URLs + commands).
Top-level key is 'providers' — name -> spec. Each provider type
has its own required field set; see docs/oauth-passthrough.md.
Default file ships ONE provider — github_oauth_app — to match the
default mcp catalog's single 'github' entry. Override by mounting
your own file at /etc/zelosmcp/auth-providers.json (k8s) or
pointing ZELOSMCP_AUTH_PROVIDERS_FILE at it (local).
Field semantics by 'type':
github_device_flow — public OAuth client, RFC 8628 device flow
against github.com. Requires client_id (the public OAuth
App identifier; safe to commit).
okta_device_flow — public OAuth client against an Okta tenant.
Requires issuer + client_id. Optional membership_hint
surfaces in the GUI as 'Membership required: ...' so users
know which Okta group they need before hitting the consent
screen.
passthrough — wraps the legacy 'forward Authorization verbatim'
behaviour. Used by backends that opt out of the broker.
static — wraps the legacy 'auth.bearer' static-token mode.
